Name: Sprigatito
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

Name: Fuecoco
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.

Name: Quaxly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime.

Name: Mimikyu ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Quaquaval
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.

Name: Pawmot
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put 1 damage counter on each of them.
Flavor-Text: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

Name: Revavroom
Ability-Effect: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Spidops
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

Name: Espathra
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

Name: Miraidon
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.

Name: Koraidon
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won’t grow.

Name: Ampharos ex
Effects: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon to have this attack do 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lucario ex
Effects: This attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cyclizar ex
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Baxcalibur
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.

Name: Tinkaton
Ability-Effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Pelipper
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 1: put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand; or search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, put it into your hand, and then shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is a flying transporter that carries small Pokémon in its beak. It bobs on the waves to rest its wings.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Tinkatink
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pikachu seems excited to be going on an adventure, and so do its new pals, from attention-seeking Sprigatito, to laid-back Fuecoco and tidy Quaxly.

Name: Miraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic [L] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Annihilape ex
Effects: Put up to 12 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: Once at the end of your turn (after your attack), you may use this Ability. Draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as a bringer of blessings, it’s been depicted on good-luck charms since ancient times.

Name: Mawile
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Pawmi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

Name: Charmander
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Paradise Resort
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Psyduck in play (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

Name: Zapdos ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [L] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put up to 2 Leftovers cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Mew ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chi-Yu
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

Name: Iron Bundle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.) If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

Name: Xatu
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Sinistea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The soul of someone who died alone possessed some leftover tea. This Pokémon appears in hotels and houses.

Name: Cetitan
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.

Name: Arctibax
Flavor-Text: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

Name: Scream Tail
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

Name: Maschiff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Metang
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic [M] Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.

Name: Bellibolt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
You may discard up to 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

Name: Flutter Mane
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no Abilities, except for Midnight Fluttering.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

Name: Iron Thorns
Effects: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 70 damage for each Future card you find there. Then, discard those Future cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Pikachu ex

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Mareep’s fluffy coat of wool rubs together and builds a static charge. The more static electricity is charged, the more brightly the lightbulb at the tip of its tail glows.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy fleece easily stores electricity. Its rubbery hide keeps it from being electrocuted.

Name: Ampharos
Flavor-Text: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

Name: Darkrai ex
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

Name: Bisharp
Flavor-Text: Bisharp mercilessly cuts its opponents to pieces with the sharp blades covering its body. It will do anything to win.

Name: Kingambit
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Picnicker
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, draw 4 cards. If tails, draw 2 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Thwackey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if your Active Pokémon has the Festival Lead Ability, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

Name: Infernape
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [R] Energy card, a Basic [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness.

Name: Froslass
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent’s), except any Froslass.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Tatsugiri
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Supporter card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

Name: Toxel
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel’s body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

Name: Revavroom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Snorlax
Flavor-Text: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

Name: Iono
Description: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws a card for each of their remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Armarouge ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 80 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Palafin ex
Ability-Effect: Put this Pokémon into play only with the effect of Palafin’s Zero to Hero Ability.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Walking Wake ex
Ability-Effect: Damage from attacks used by this Pokémon isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Leaves ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, you may move any amount of Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pecharunt
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put 5 more damage counters on your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.

Name: Kingambit
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has 4 or more damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: Put up to 3 [W] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
This attack does 50 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greninja ex
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ledian
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has 90 HP or less remaining to the Active Spot.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

Name: Crabominable
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by this Pokémon cost [C] less for each Kofu card in your discard pile.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Haymaker.
Flavor-Text: Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon’s movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Drifloon and Drifblim in play. This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Drifloon and Drifblim. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can’t be a fixed destination.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: As long as you have at least 1 other Bouffalant in play, all of your Basic [C] Pokémon take 60 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Curly Wall doesn’t stack.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: They swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After artificial intelligence was implemented in Porygon2, the Pokémon began using a strange language that only other Porygon2 understand.

Name: Latias
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

Name: Tinkaton
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

Name: Noctowl
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, if you have any Tera Pokémon in play, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its extremely soft wings make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.

Name: Victini ex
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon ex
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Attach a Basic [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gouging Fire ex
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Blaze Blitz again until it leaves the Active Spot.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Raging Bolt ex
Effects: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.
You may discard any amount of Basic Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Crown ex
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Future Pokémon, except any Iron Crown ex, do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on those Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iron Boulder ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon
Flavor-Text: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

Name: Pecharunt
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put 5 more damage counters on your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.

Name: Paradise Resort
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Psyduck in play (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Gouging Fire
Effects: If your opponent has 4 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.

Name: Chien-Pao
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Stadium in play.
Effects: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

Name: Magneton
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 3 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your [L] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: They’re formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Indeedee
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon and have it recover from a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer’s side. It predicts their actions with its psychic power and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

Name: Wooper
Effects: Shuffle up to 3 Basic [W] Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn’t care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Attach a number of Basic [L] Energy cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Magneton
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 3 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your [L] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: They’re formed by several Magnemite linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Squawkabilly ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may discard your hand and draw 6 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Squawk and Seize Ability during your turn.
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Houndstone ex
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Flare Strike.
This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Lapras ex
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Look at the top 20 cards of your deck and attach any number of Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Terapagos ex
Effects: If you go second, you can’t use this attack during your first turn. This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic non-[C] Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Flareon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid’s.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Attach a Basic [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal all damage from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

Name: Sylveon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, it can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Eevee ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can evolve into any Pokémon ex that evolves from Eevee if you play it from your hand onto this Pokémon. (This Pokémon can’t evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Espeon ex
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Devolve each of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon by shuffling the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Umbreon ex
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and take a Prize card.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Bloodmoon Ursaluna ex
Ability-Effect: Blood Moon used by this Pokémon costs [C] less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyogre ex
Effects: You may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Xerneas ex
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dialga ex
Effects: Draw a card.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: N’s Darmanitan
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Basic Energy card in your opponent’s discard pile.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Iono’s Kilowattrel
Ability-Effect: You must discard a Basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

Name: Lillie’s Ribombee
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand and you put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto their Bench.
Flavor-Text: Ribombee can sense when people or Pokémon are feeling down, and it will cheer them up with handmade pollen puffs.

Name: Hop’s Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your Hop’s Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). The effect of Extra Helpings doesn’t stack.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 80 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This gluttonous Pokémon eats constantly, apart from when it’s asleep. It devours nearly 900 pounds of food per day.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: It can see in all directions without moving its big eyes, helping it spot both attackers and prey right away.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: If it locks eyes with you, watch out! Nothing and no one is safe from the reckless headbutts of this troublesome Pokémon.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of your Benched Pokémon and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: As halfhearted as this Pokémon’s kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr’s concrete pillars.

Name: N’s Zorua
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Sprigatito
Flavor-Text: Liko’s partner is quirky and has its own tempo. No one really knows what this Sprigatito is thinking.

Name: Fuecoco
Flavor-Text: It lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.

Name: Hop’s Zacian ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Brave Slash.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iono’s Bellibolt ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [L] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Iono’s Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lillie’s Clefairy ex
Ability-Effect: The Weakness of each of your opponent’s [N] Pokémon in play is now [P]. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Koraidon ex
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zacian ex
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [M] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic [G] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can evolve into a variety of forms. Eevee’s genes are the key to solving the mysteries of Pokémon evolution.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: Discard your hand and draw 6 cards.
Flavor-Text: Once the herd hears thunder, it chases after the storm clouds so that the Blitzle in the group can use the lightning to charge up.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.

Name: Team Rocket’s Wobbuffet
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Team Rocket’s Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

Name: Cynthia’s Garchomp ex
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Team Rocket’s Mewtwo ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t attack unless you have 4 or more Team Rocket’s Pokémon in play.
Effects: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Marnie’s Morpeko
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Morpeko eats seeds constantly to keep its hunger at bay. If it’s hungry for too long, it grows wild and brutish.

Name: Steven’s Beldum
Flavor-Text: The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground’s natural magnetism, letting it float.

Name: Victini
Effects: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Move a Basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Gothitelle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 3 cards.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Toxtricity ex
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Team Rocket’s Mewtwo ex

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Team Rocket’s Persian ex
Effects: Reveal the top 10 cards of your opponent’s deck. You may choose an attack from a Pokémon you find there and use it as this attack. Shuffle the revealed cards into your opponent’s deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Black Belt’s Training
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 40 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Paradise Resort
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Psyduck in play (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.
